title: TypeScript, JavaScript, and the TypeScript 7 Toolchain · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: Trace a service-status module through TypeScript 7 checking, JavaScript emission, module loading, and runtime behavior. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a compiler-to-runtime evidence notebook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish compile-time type evidence from emitted JavaScript and runtime validation.
Predict which TypeScript constructs erase, transform, or affect emitted JavaScript and source maps.
Use the TypeScript 7 native compiler and explain when a TypeScript 6 API compatibility tool remains necessary.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.typescript:
// typescript-basics cumulative strict TypeScript portfolio.
// Add one typed behavior, runtime boundary, changed case, and evidence return per activity.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Static Runtime Boundary evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Static Runtime Boundary assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: A quiz build checks a two-package TypeScript 7 workspace with strict mode, ESM output, and one TypeScript 6 API-dependent tool. Record the emitted JavaScript, diagnostic code, runtime result, and the compatibility boundary before release 4.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Static Runtime Boundary has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: Name the Static Runtime Boundary precondition or invariant that the quiz case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: A quiz build checks a two-package TypeScript 7 workspace with strict mode, ESM output, and one TypeScript 6 API-dependent tool. Record the emitted JavaScript, diagnostic code, runtime result, and the compatibility boundary before release 4.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: A passing type check proves every runtime input is valid.

options.text: Declare the domain relationship and identify every untrusted runtime boundary
Infer or annotate the smallest sound type and validate unknown values before narrowing
Run strict diagnostics, inspect emitted JavaScript, and execute the changed case
Add compile-time rejection and runtime boundary evidence, then document remaining limits
